functions_added: normalize_content(filepath, frontmatter, body, normalize=True) -> tuple[dict, str]

cli_additions.description: Apply content normalization (trafilatura extraction, boilerplate removal, summary generation)

heuristic_strip_boilerplate.details: Removes navigation, skip links, footer boilerplate; preserves content

generate_summary.details: Extracts first meaningful paragraph (>80 chars), truncates at sentence boundaries

classify_ingest_mode.details: Correctly classifies social, profile, article, resource, link modes

extract_with_trafilatura.details: 274KB HTML -> 3.5KB text (98.7% reduction), extracts title/author/date metadata

full_pipeline_dry_run.details: Dry run on existing file works; normalization integrated into ingest_file

notes_for_orchestrator: Normalization is now ON by default for all ingests
Without HTML companion files, normalization is limited to heuristic stripping (mild improvement)
With HTML companion files, trafilatura achieves 98%+ reduction with excellent metadata extraction
Summary generation uses simple heuristics (first meaningful paragraph) - LLM fallback can be added later
The auto-ingest user rule should be updated to remove explicit --type since we now auto-detect
Social posts (x.com, twitter.com, linkedin.com/posts) are preserved as-is (no heuristic stripping)

integration_points.ingest_file: Now accepts normalize=True parameter, applies normalization before metadata extraction
